By providing undeniable upfront value, such as a custom mini-audit or a short loom video pointing out easy wins, you position yourself as a consultative expert rather than a desperate salesperson.

Use the templates below to structure your agency outreach. Whether you are pitching website redesigns, outbound lead generation, or advanced Google Ads management, these customized templates will significantly boost your meeting booking rate.
templates:
Subject: Quick idea regarding [Company Name]'s SEO strategy

Hi [Name],

I was doing some research on the [Industry] space in [City] and noticed that [Competitor Name] is currently outranking [Company Name] for 'Best [Service] in [City]'.

I ran a quick technical audit on your site and found three easily fixable errors that are likely holding you back from that top spot. 

I recorded a 3-minute video showing exactly what these errors are and how your developer can fix them today. Would you like me to send the link over?

Best,
[Your Name]

Subject: Is your current Google Ads budget being wasted?

Hi [Name],

I noticed [Company Name] is currently running Google Ads for the keyword '[Specific Keyword]'. 

However, I clicked through and saw the ad points directly to your homepage rather than a dedicated, high-converting landing page. In my experience managing ad spend for [Industry] companies, this typically reduces conversion rates by at least [X]%.

At [Your Agency], we help businesses optimize their ad spend by building custom funnels. Would you be open to a 10-minute chat to see how we could lower your Cost Per Acquisition?

Cheers,
[Your Name]

Subject: [Company Name]’s social media presence

Hi [Name],

I’ve been following [Company Name]’s recent product launches and love the direction the brand is heading.

I did notice that while your upcoming products are highly visual, your Instagram engagement has been relatively flat over the last quarter. My agency recently helped a similar brand, [Similar Brand], increase their organic social engagement by [X]% in under 60 days by implementing a [Specific Strategy] strategy.

If improving social ROI is a priority for you this quarter, I’d love to share some quick ideas. Are you free next Tuesday?

Best regards,
[Your Name]
tips: Always offer an upfront piece of customized value, such as a mini-audit or a Loom video.
Avoid using spammy marketing buzzwords like 'guaranteed ROI' or 'page 1 in 24 hours'.
Keep the email short; the goal is to get permission to send the video or book a call.

faq.question: Should I attach a full PDF audit to the first email?
faq.answer: No. Attachments can trigger spam filters and overwhelm the prospect. Ask for permission first.

faq.question: How personalized does a marketing pitch need to be?
faq.answer: Highly personalized. If they feel you copy-pasted the email to 500 other businesses, they will delete it immediately.

faq.question: Is a Loom video better than text?
faq.answer: Yes, personalized Loom videos significantly boost reply rates in digital marketing outreach by proving you researched their specific site.

title: Influencer Outreach Email Templates
description: Partner with top creators using these professional influencer outreach and brand collaboration email templates.
intro:
Influencer marketing has evolved into a highly competitive landscape. Top creators and micro-influencers alike receive dozens of collaboration requests daily. If your outreach email is overly demanding, unclear about compensation, or demonstrates that you haven't actually engaged with their content, your email will be ignored. To successfully recruit influencers for your brand campaigns, your outreach must be personalized, deeply respectful of their time, and clear regarding the mutual benefits.

A great influencer outreach email establishes immediate rapport by referencing a specific, recent piece of their content. It then seamlessly transitions into why they are the perfect fit for your specific brand or upcoming campaign. Crucially, the best outreach emails are transparent. While you don't need to negotiate the final contract in the first email, you should be clear if this is a paid opportunity, a gifted product collaboration, or an affiliate partnership. Influencers are professionals; respect their business model.

These templates cover various influencer marketing scenarios, including initial contact, sending a gifted product, or negotiating a long-term brand ambassadorship. Customize the variables to ensure your brand stands out in a crowded inbox.
templates:
Subject: Collaboration inquiry: [Your Brand] x [Influencer Name]

Hi [Name],

My name is [Your Name] and I’m reaching out from [Your Brand]. I absolutely loved your recent video regarding [Specific Topic]; your take on [Specific Detail] was spot on.

Because your aesthetic and focus on [Niche] aligns so perfectly with our brand, we would love to partner with you for our upcoming [Season/Product] campaign.

This would be a paid collaboration. Would you be open to discussing this further? If so, please let me know or send over your media kit and current rates.

Best,
[Your Name]

Subject: We’d love to send you some [Product Type]!

Hi [Name],

I’ve been following your page for a while and your recent posts on [Topic] have been fantastic. 

I’m with [Your Brand], and we are launching a new line of [Product]. We think you would absolutely love it, and we’d love to send you a PR package—no strings attached or posting requirements.

If you’re interested in trying it out, just reply with your preferred shipping address and any specific sizing/color preferences!

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Subject: Brand Ambassadorship with [Your Brand]

Hi [Name],

I’ll keep this brief! I’m managing partnerships for [Your Brand], and we are currently recruiting long-term brand ambassadors who are deeply passionate about [Industry/Niche].

Given your highly engaged audience and fantastic content style, you are exactly who we are looking to partner with. Our ambassador program includes [Briefly list 2 benefits, e.g., monthly paid deliverables and exclusive affiliate rates].

If you have the bandwidth for a new long-term partnership right now, we’d love to hop on a quick 10-minute call to discuss the details. 

Let me know your thoughts!

Cheers,
[Your Name]
tips: Always use their actual name, not their social media handle.
Be clear upfront if it is a paid gig or a gifted exchange to avoid wasting their time.
Reference a specific piece of content to prove you aren't just mass-blasting a list.

faq.question: Should I ask for a media kit in the first email?
faq.answer: Yes, if it's a paid collaboration, asking for their media kit or rate card is completely standard and expected.

faq.question: How do I follow up with an influencer?
faq.answer: Give them at least 4 to 5 days to respond before sending a polite, short follow-up email. They are often traveling or balancing tight content schedules.

faq.question: Is it okay to DM them instead of emailing?
faq.answer: Email is significantly more professional for business inquiries. Only use a DM to ask for the best email address to send a collaboration proposal to.

title: Guest Post Pitch Email Templates for SEO
description: Secure high-quality backlinks and guest posting opportunities with these proven blogger outreach templates.
intro:
Securing high-quality guest posts is a cornerstone of any robust SEO and content marketing strategy. However, editors and blog managers at high-Domain Authority (DA) websites are inundated with terrible, spammy guest post pitches every single day. Most pitches are swiftly deleted because they offer zero value to the host blog, propose topics that have already been covered, or demand a backlink without offering high-quality content in return.

To get your guest post pitch accepted, you must do your homework. A winning pitch requires you to study their blog, identify a "content gap" (a topic relevant to their audience that they haven't covered recently), and propose 2 to 3 highly specific, compelling headlines. Your email must quickly establish your credibility as an expert writer by linking to previously published, high-quality work. Furthermore, you should make it explicitly clear that your content will be 100% original, incredibly well-researched, and tailored specifically to their audience's needs.

These customized outreach templates are designed to help you bypass the spam folder and secure valuable publishing spots. Adapting these templates to reflect genuine research into the target blog will drastically improve your guest posting acceptance rates.
templates:
Subject: Guest post proposal: 3 ideas for [Blog Name]'s audience

Hi [Editor's Name],

I’m a long-time reader of [Blog Name] and absolutely loved your recent deep-dive into [Recent Article Topic]. 

I’m reaching out because I’d love to contribute a high-quality guest post to your site. Based on what resonates with your audience, I’ve brainstormed three potential topics that haven't been covered recently:

1. [Compelling Headline Idea 1]
2. [Compelling Headline Idea 2]
3. [Compelling Headline Idea 3]

For context on my writing style, I recently published similar comprehensive guides on [High DA Site 1] and [High DA Site 2].

Do any of these topics sound like a good fit for your editorial calendar?

Best,
[Your Name]

Subject: Broken link on your [Topic] post + A quick content idea

Hi [Name],

I was doing some research on [Topic] today and came across your excellent article: [Link to their article]. 

While reading, I noticed that the link pointing to [Broken Link Details] is unfortunately returning a 404 error. Just wanted to give you a heads-up so you can fix it!

Also, since your audience clearly cares about [Topic], I recently wrote a highly detailed, 2,000-word guide on [Related Topic]. Would you be open to me writing a customized version of this specifically as a guest post for your site?

Thanks for all the great content,
[Your Name]

Subject: Following up on my guest post pitch to [Blog Name]

Hi [Editor's Name],

I’m writing to quickly follow up on the guest post ideas I sent over last Tuesday. 

I know managing an editorial calendar is incredibly time-consuming. If none of those three topics were the right fit, I’d be more than happy to brainstorm alternative ideas that better align with your current content goals for Q3.

Just let me know if you are currently accepting new contributors!

Best regards,
[Your Name]
tips: Always pitch 2 or 3 distinct headlines so the editor has options.
Link to a few of your best previously published articles to prove your writing quality.
Never submit a pre-written article in the first email; pitch ideas first.

faq.question: How long should I wait to follow up on a guest post pitch?
faq.answer: Editors are incredibly busy. Wait at least one full week before sending a single polite follow-up.

faq.question: Should I mention that I want a backlink?
faq.answer: It's generally understood that guest posts include a byline bio link. There's usually no need to aggressively demand it in the initial friendly pitch.

faq.question: What if they ask for payment to publish?
faq.answer: This is known as a 'sponsored post'. Depending on your strategy and budget, you must decide if paying for a link aligns with your SEO goals and Google's guidelines.

title: Cold Email Templates for SEO Agencies
description: Generate highly qualified leads for your SEO agency with these targeted reach-out templates focused on organic growth and technical audits.
intro:
Selling SEO services requires overcoming a massive hurdle: skepticism. Because the barrier to entry for SEO consulting is low, business owners are bombarded by automated, low-quality emails promising them the number one spot on Google overnight. To successfully sell high-ticket SEO retainers, your cold outreach must instantly separate you from the spammers by demonstrating profound, undeniable expertise specific to the prospect's actual website.

The most effective SEO cold emails are heavily customized and value-driven. You must identify a specific, quantifiable problem with their current organic presence—such as a critical technical error, a massive keyword gap compared to their direct competitor, or a catastrophic drop in site speed. By highlighting this specific error and offering a completely free, no-obligation video walkthrough of how to fix it, you trigger curiosity and build immediate trust. Your goal is not to sell a $5,000 monthly retainer in the first email; your goal is simply to get them to watch your 3-minute audit video or agree to a brief discovery call.

Utilize these proven SEO agency templates to structure your outreach. Remember to keep the focus entirely on the revenue and traffic they are losing rather than the technical jargon of how exactly you will fix it.
templates:
Subject: [Company Name]'s drop in organic traffic for '[Keyword]'

Hi [Name],

I run an SEO agency specializing in the [Industry] space. I was doing some competitor analysis today and noticed that [Company Name] recently lost its page 1 ranking for the term '[Important Keyword]'.

Currently, [Competitor Name] is capturing almost all of that organic traffic. I ran a quick technical audit on your homepage and found a specific indexing error that is likely causing this drop.

I recorded a 3-minute video showing your developer exactly how to fix it today. Would you like me to send over the link?

Best,
[Your Name]

Subject: Quick question about your recent site migration

Hi [Name],

It looks like [Company Name] recently underwent a website redesign. The new layout looks fantastic!

However, I noticed that several of your highest-traffic legacy URLs were not correctly 301 redirected during the launch. This is currently resulting in massive 404 errors that are actively draining your organic authority and costing you leads.

We specialize in SEO triage and recovery. Would you be open to a brief 10-minute call next week so I can show you exactly which pages are broken and how to salvage the traffic?

Cheers,
[Your Name]

Subject: Idea for overtaking [Competitor Name] on Google

Hi [Name],

I imagine you get a lot of marketing emails, so I will be direct.

I’ve been tracking search visibility in the [City] area for [Service Type]. Right now, [Competitor Name] is generating roughly [X] more organic visits per month than [Company Name] solely because of a massive content gap regarding [Specific Topic].

I’ve mapped out a 3-month roadmap detailing exactly how you can close that gap and capture that lost revenue. 

Are you available for a 10-minute chat next Wednesday to review the roadmap?

Best regards,
[Your Name]
tips: Focus intensely on their competitors. Business owners hate losing to a specific local competitor.
Translate SEO metrics into business metrics; talk about lost revenue and leads, not just 'traffic'.
Using a customized Loom video audit is the single highest-converting strategy in SEO outreach.

faq.question: Should I attach an automated PDF SEO report?
faq.answer: No. Automated reports from tools like SEMrush or Ahrefs look like spam. A short, customized screen recording is infinitely better.

faq.question: How do I find issues to pitch?
faq.answer: Look for businesses currently running Google Ads (proving they have budget) but ranking poorly organically on page 2 or 3.

faq.question: Who is the best person to email at a larger company?
faq.answer: Target the Director of Marketing, the CMO, or the VP of Digital Strategy rather than the CEO.

title: Public Relations Pitch Emails for Journalists
description: Get your story covered. Use these PR pitch email templates to gracefully contact journalists and secure media coverage.
intro:
Securing earned media coverage requires pitching journalists who are historically overworked and subjected to hundreds of terrible PR pitches every day. The vast majority of media pitches fail because they read like corporate press releases, lack a compelling narrative angle, or are blasted to journalists who do not even cover that specific "beat." To successfully pitch a journalist, your email must be hyper-relevant, newsworthy, and incredibly concise.

A winning PR pitch immediately answers the journalist's core question: "Why does my audience care about this right now?" You must connect your company's announcement, product, or data study to a larger, timely trend occurring in the news cycle. Furthermore, you must make the journalist's job as easy as possible. Provide them with the core statistics, offer an exclusive interview with your founder, and clearly link to a media kit containing high-resolution assets.

The templates below are tailored for different PR scenarios, such as offering an exclusive embargo, pitching a data-driven story, or offering a subject matter expert for an ongoing breaking news cycle. Use them to build respectful, mutually beneficial relationships with the media.
templates:
Subject: EXCLUSIVE: New data on [Timely Trend/Topic]

Hi [Journalist's Name],

I loved your recent piece on [Recent Article Topic]; your analysis of [Specific Detail] was incredibly insightful.

Given your focus on [Their Beat/Niche], I wanted to offer you an exclusive first look at a new data study from [Your Company] regarding [Related Topic]. 

We analyzed over [Number] data points and found a massive shift in consumer behavior:
- [Fascinating Stat 1]
- [Fascinating Stat 2]

I can offer you an exclusive on this data until [Date/Time], as well as an interview with our CEO to discuss the implications for the industry. 

Let me know if you are interested in covering this!

Best,
[Your Name]

Subject: PITCH: How [Company] is solving [Industry Problem]

Hi [Name],

As you’ve covered extensively recently, the [Industry] sector is currently grappling with a massive shortage of [Specific Resource/Issue]. 

Next Tuesday, [Your Company] is officially coming out of stealth to announce a $[Amount] Seed round to solve this exact bottleneck using [Brief explanation of technology]. 

Unlike [Competitor], we are approaching the problem by [Key Differentiator]. 

I have attached our press release under embargo until [Date]. Would you be interested in a brief interview with our founder prior to the launch?

Cheers,
[Your Name]

Subject: Expert Source available for your coverage on [Breaking News Topic]

Hi [Name],

I’m following your ongoing coverage of [Breaking News Event]. 

If you need an expert source to provide context on the technical implications of this event, [Expert's Name], the [Title] at [Your Company], is available for immediate interviews via phone or Zoom today.

[Expert's Name] has over 15 years of experience in [Niche] and recently authored a paper predicting this exact outcome.

Please let me know if you’d like to arrange a quick call or if you need written commentary.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
tips: Respect their 'beat'. Never pitch a tech journalist a story about a new fashion line.
Use bullet points to highlight the most fascinating stats or takeaways immediately.
Offer 'Exclusives' or 'Embargos' to give top-tier publications an incentive to cover it first.

faq.question: What is an embargo?
faq.answer: An embargo is an agreement that the journalist will not publish the story until a specific, agreed-upon date and time.

faq.question: Should I paste the press release in the email?
faq.answer: It is better to write a short, compelling 3-paragraph pitch and link out to a Google Doc containing the full press release and media assets.

faq.question: How do I follow up on a PR pitch?
faq.answer: Send one extremely short follow-up 24 to 48 hours later. If there is no response, they are passing on the story. Move on.

title: Subscription Cancellation Email Templates (Try to Save Them)
description: Reduce churn with these tested cancellation autoresponders and win-back email templates for SaaS and subscription businesses.
intro:
Customer churn is the silent killer of subscription and SaaS businesses. When a user requests to cancel their account, the immediate response they receive can be the difference between losing them forever and successfully saving the account. While you must never make the cancellation process overly difficult or frustrating—which will only result in furious customers and chargebacks—you absolutely should use the cancellation confirmation email as a final, polite attempt to win them back or pause their billing.

An effective cancellation or churn-save email must immediately confirm that their request has been successfully processed, relieving their anxiety. Only after confirming the cancellation should you deploy your win-back strategy. This could involve offering a steep temporary discount, suggesting a pause instead of a cancellation, or asking for critical feedback through a brief survey. Presenting these options gracefully shows that you value their business and respect their time.

The templates below cover the most common subscription cancellation scenarios, from acknowledging a voluntary churn to executing an automated win-back sequence weeks after they have left. By standardizing these emails, you can passively recover a significant percentage of lost revenue.
templates:
Subject: Your [Company Name] subscription has been cancelled

Hi [Name],

As requested, your [Product/Plan Name] subscription has been completely cancelled. You will not be billed again, and you will continue to have full access to your account until the end of your billing cycle on [Date].

We are incredibly sad to see you go! Before you leave, could I ask you a quick favor? 

If you have 30 seconds, please reply to this email and let me know why you decided to cancel. Your feedback goes directly to our product team and helps us improve the platform for everyone.

If you ever need us in the future, we will be right here.

Best wishes,
[Your Name] & The [Company Name] Team

Subject: We've processed your cancellation (Quick Question inside)

Hi [Name],

Your cancellation request has been successfully processed. 

However, I noticed you have been a loyal customer with us for the last [Time Period], and frankly, we hate to lose you! Often, users cancel because they simply aren't utilizing the platform enough right now.

Did you know we can simply 'pause' your account for 3 months instead? You won't be charged a dime, and your data will remain perfectly intact for when you return.

If you'd like me to pause your account instead, just reply 'Pause' to this email and I’ll handle the rest. Otherwise, no further action is needed on your end.

Warmly,
[Your Name]

Subject: We miss you! Here is [X]% off to come back

Hi [Name],

It’s been a few months since you left [Company Name], and our product team has been incredibly busy rolling out new features! 

Since you left, we have completely overhauled [Feature A] and introduced [Feature B], making the platform faster and more intuitive than ever.

We’d love for you to give us another try. If you reactivate your account this week, you can use the promo code WELCOMEBACK to get [X]% off your next three months.

Click here to reactivate your account: [Link]

Hope to see you back!

Best,
[Your Name]
tips: Never bury the confirmation. Immediately restate that they are successfully cancelled so they feel secure.
Make the feedback request as frictionless as possible (e.g., asking them to just reply to the email).
Offering to 'pause' rather than cancel is one of the highest-converting save techniques in SaaS.

faq.question: Is it legal to require an email to cancel?
faq.answer: Regulations vary by region (e.g., California has strict 'click to cancel' laws for online subscriptions). It's best practice to allow in-app cancellations and use the confirmation email to win them back.

faq.question: When should I send a win-back email?
faq.answer: Usually 30, 60, and 90 days post-cancellation. Timing aligns beautifully with their potential realization that they miss your software.

faq.question: Should I offer a discount in the initial cancellation email?
faq.answer: Yes, many companies offer an automated 50% discount for the next month immediately upon clicking cancel to retain price-sensitive users.

title: Customer Feedback and Review Request Emails
description: Generate more reviews and collect valuable product insights with these high-converting customer feedback email templates.
intro:
In the modern digital economy, positive reviews and actionable customer feedback are the bedrock of growth. Prospective buyers rely heavily on peer reviews before making purchasing decisions, and your product team desperately needs raw customer sentiment to guide their roadmap. However, customers are incredibly busy. Without a clear, compelling, and timely email request, even your happiest customers will rarely go out of their way to leave a review.

The secret to an effective feedback request email is timing and simplicity. You should ideally trigger the email shortly after the customer has experienced "first value"—for instance, two days after their product is delivered, or immediately after a successful customer support interaction. The email itself must be overwhelmingly simple. Explain exactly how long the request will take (e.g., "Just one click" or "30 seconds") and provide a massive, direct link to the survey or review platform.

Use the customized templates below to streamline your feedback collection process. Whether you are aiming to boost your Net Promoter Score (NPS), generate G2/Capterra reviews for your software, or simply request a quick testimonial, these templates maximize response rates while respecting your customer’s time.
templates:
Subject: How are you loving your [Product Name]?

Hi [Name],

I saw that your [Product Name] was delivered a few days ago, and I wanted to personally reach out to make sure everything arrived perfectly!

If you have 60 seconds today, we would absolutely love it if you could share your thoughts on the product by leaving a quick review. Your feedback not only helps our small team grow, but it helps other customers make informed choices.

You can click here to leave a quick 5-star review: [Link to Review Platform]

Thank you so much for your support!

Warmly,
[Your Name]

Subject: A quick favor? (Takes 30 seconds)

Hi [Name],

It’s been a few months since your team upgraded to the [Plan Tier] plan with [Company Name]. We’re thrilled to have you on board!

We are currently gathering feedback from our power users, and I wanted to ask a quick favor. Would you mind leaving a brief, honest review of our software on [Review Site, e.g., G2 or Capterra]? 

As a thank you for your time, we will send you a $20 Amazon Gift Card as soon as the review is published.

You can leave your review here: [Link]

Thanks in advance,
[Your Name]

Subject: How did we do today? 

Hi [Name],

Your recent support ticket regarding [Ticket Topic] has been successfully closed by our team.

We are constantly striving to improve our customer support experience. Would you mind answering just one question for us?

How would you rate the support you received today?
[Link: Amazing] | [Link: Good] | [Link: Poor]

Thanks for helping us improve!

Best,
The [Company Name] Support Team
tips: Incentivize long-form software reviews (like G2 or Capterra) with gift cards, as they take significant time.
Embedded one-click NPS surveys (1-10 scores) directly within the email yield the highest engagement.
Send review requests from a person's name, not a generic 'noreply@company.com'.

faq.question: Is it legal to offer incentives for reviews?
faq.answer: On platforms like G2 or Capterra, offering a nominal gift card for an 'honest' review is standard. However, offering incentives for Google or Amazon reviews violates their terms of service.

faq.question: What is a good response rate for a feedback email?
faq.answer: For simple NPS emails, 10-20% is strong. For long-form written reviews, 2-5% is typical without incentives.

faq.question: When is the best time to ask a SaaS customer for a review?
faq.answer: Wait until after they have hit a positive milestone, such as renewing their contract or successfully completing their onboarding.

title: SaaS Subscription Renewal Reminder Emails
description: Ensure smooth yearly renewals with these proactive SaaS renewal reminder templates. Reduce involuntary churn effectively.
intro:
Annual subscription renewals represent a critical revenue moment for any B2B SaaS company. If an enterprise client is surprised by a massive auto-renewal charge because they weren't adequately notified, the resulting friction can permanently damage the relationship and lead to immediate churn. Professionalism dictates that you manage renewals gracefully, clearly communicating upcoming charges while simultaneously reiterating the value the client has received over the past year.

A perfectly structured renewal sequence usually begins 30 to 45 days before the contract end date. This early notification allows the client ample time to secure internal budget approvals and communicate with their procurement team. The email should clearly state the renewal date, the amount to be charged, and crucially, highlight a few key metrics demonstrating the ROI they achieved using your platform over the last twelve months. It is also the optimal time to pitch an upgrade or a multi-year discount.

Below are standard renewal templates customized for different stages of the contract lifecycle. Use them to ensure transparency, prevent involuntary churn due to expired credit cards, and open positive dialogues about account expansion.
templates:
Subject: Upcoming Renewal Notice for [Company Name]

Hi [Name],

I can’t believe it’s already been a year! I’m writing to let you know that your annual subscription to [Product Name] is scheduled to automatically renew on [Date].

Over the past year, your team successfully processed over [Metric/Number] tasks using our platform. We look forward to supporting your growth even further in the coming year.

The card on file ending in [Last 4 Digits] will be charged $[Amount] on the renewal date. If you need to update your billing information or adjust your plan tier, you can do so securely here: [Link].

Please let me know if you have any questions or if you need an updated invoice for your procurement team.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Subject: Action Required: Your payment method is expiring

Hi [Name],

We noticed that the credit card associated with your [Product Name] account is set to expire soon. 

To ensure there is no disruption to your service when your account renews on [Date], please take a brief moment to update your billing details.

You can securely update your payment method directly in your billing dashboard here: [Link].

If you need any assistance, please reply directly to this email and our support team will help you right away.

Best,
The [Company Name] Billing Team

Subject: Let’s review your [Company Name] contract

Hi [Name],

Your [Product Name] enterprise contract is up for renewal in roughly 60 days on [Date].

Before we simply process the renewal, I’d love to schedule a brief 15-minute Account Review. I want to ensure my team is doing everything possible to support your upcoming Q3 initiatives, and I’d also love to show you a few new features we are launching next month.

Lastly, if your team is planning to expand usage this year, we can discuss locking in a multi-year discount.

Do you have 15 minutes available next week?

Cheers,
[Your Name]
tips: Always send the first renewal notice at least 30 to 45 days in advance for B2B contracts.
Use the renewal notice as an opportunity to schedule a strategic account review call.
Clearly indicate the last 4 digits of the card on file so they know exactly which payment method will be used.

faq.question: Is sending a renewal reminder legally required?
faq.answer: In many jurisdictions, specifically for consumer subscriptions or auto-renewing annual contracts, sending a clear renewal notification is legally required by consumer protection laws.

faq.question: What is 'involuntary churn'?
faq.answer: Involuntary churn happens when a customer's account is cancelled purely due to payment failure, such as an expired credit card, rather than a desire to cancel.

faq.question: How many Dunning (payment failure) emails should I send?
faq.answer: A typical sequence includes 3-4 emails spread over 14 days, accompanied by in-app notifications, before freezing the account.

title: Product Update & Feature Announcement Emails
description: Excite your user base and drive adoption with these engaging product update and feature launch email templates.
intro:
Launching a new feature is exciting, but simply pushing code to production doesn't guarantee that anyone will actually use it. To drive rapid adoption and remind your existing user base of your platform's evolving value, you must craft compelling feature announcement emails. A poor product update email reads like an internal technical changelog; a great one reads like a solution to a frustrating problem.

The most successful feature announcements focus heavily on the "Why" and the "How." Don't just proudly announce that you built a new data export API. Instead, focus on the user benefit: "You can now export your compliance data seamlessly in one click, saving you hours of manual reporting." The email should be highly visual, ideally including a vibrant GIF or screenshot showcasing the new UI, and must contain a crystal clear Call to Action (CTA) directing the user straight into the app to try the feature out themselves.

These templates offer tailored messaging for different types of product launches—from major version overhauls to highly requested quality-of-life improvements. Use them to ensure your engineering team’s hard work gets the attention and adoption it deserves.
templates:
Subject: You asked, we built it: Introducing [New Feature]

Hi [Name],

Over the last few months, the number one request we’ve received from teams like yours is the ability to [Perform Specific Action]. 

Today, we are incredibly excited to announce that [New Feature] is officially live and available in your account!

Instead of dealing with [Old frustrating process], you can now use [New Feature] to instantly [Benefit/Outcome]. It’s designed to save you hours of manual work every single week.

[Insert vibrant GIF or Screenshot here]

Ready to try it out? Click below to log in and configure your new workflow.

[Button: Try out New Feature]

Best,
The [Company Name] Product Team

Subject: Huge updates inside: the new [Company Name] Dashboard

Hi [Name],

Welcome to an entirely new [Company Name] experience. 

Over the weekend, we rolled out a massive overhaul to our core reporting dashboard. We’ve redesigned the entire interface from the ground up to make finding your most important data faster, highly customizable, and vastly more beautiful.

Here’s what’s new:
- 🚀 Lightning Fast: Load times have been reduced by [X]%.
- 📊 Custom Widgets: Pin your favorite KPIs directly to the top.
- 🌙 Dark Mode: Because you asked for it.

Log in today to explore the new dashboard and let us know what you think!

[Button: View My New Dashboard]

Cheers,
[Your Name]

Subject: Beta Invitation: Early access to [Upcoming Feature]

Hi [Name],

Because you are one of our most active power users, we wanted to extend an exclusive invitation to you.

Our engineering team is currently putting the final touches on [Upcoming Feature], a highly anticipated tool that will allow you to [Core Benefit]. Before we release it to the public next month, we are opening up a private Beta to a select group of customers.

If you want early access to test it out and help shape the final functionality, just reply 'Yes' to this email and we’ll flip the switch on your account.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
tips: Always include an animated GIF or a high-quality screenshot to visually demonstrate the feature.
Frame the announcement around the problem it solves, not just the technical specifications.
If the feature fulfills a highly upvoted feature request, explicitly state 'You asked, we listened' to build community goodwill.

faq.question: Should I send feature announcements to inactive users?
faq.answer: Yes! A major feature launch is an excellent 'win-back' strategy for users who churned due to missing functionality.

faq.question: How often should I send product updates?
faq.answer: Wait until you have a major feature, or aggregate smaller updates into a monthly or quarterly 'Product Round-Up' newsletter.

faq.question: Are emojis appropriate in announcement subject lines?
faq.answer: Absolutely. Emojis (like 🚀 or ✨) often boost open rates for product update emails by making them stand out as exciting news.

title: Apology Emails for Billing and Payment Errors
description: Handle sensitive payment issues professionally with these graceful apology email templates for billing and invoice errors.
intro:
Few things damage client trust faster than a mistake involving their money. Whether your system accidentally double-charged a client, sent an incorrect invoice, or failed to apply an agreed-upon discount, a billing error requires precise, immediate communication. You cannot wait for the client to discover the error on their bank statement; you must proactively inform them, apologize sincerely without making excuses, and detail exactly how and when their funds will be corrected.

A professional billing apology email must be strictly factual and deeply reassuring. Customers panic when they see unexpected charges. Therefore, your email must immediately confirm that the error has already been identified and that the refund or credit represents fixing it immediately. Avoid lengthy technical explanations about why the payment gateway glitched; the client only cares that their money is safe and the issue is resolved.

Use the customized templates below to gracefully navigate stressful billing mishaps. They are designed to convey urgent priority, maintain transparency, and ultimately reinforce to the client that your company operates with total integrity.
templates:
Subject: Important: Correction to your recent [Company Name] invoice

Dear [Name],

I am writing to sincerely apologize for a billing error on your account today. 

Due to a processing glitch in our system, you were mistakenly charged twice for your monthly subscription of $[Amount]. 

We caught the error immediately, and I have already issued a full refund of the duplicate charge back to your original payment method. Depending on your bank, it may take 3-5 business days for the funds to reflect on your statement.

I am incredibly sorry for any panic or inconvenience this caused. We have applied a patch to our billing software to ensure this does not happen again.

Please let me know if you need any further documentation or a corrected invoice.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

Subject: Apology regarding your missing discount

Hi [Name],

I’m reaching out because I noticed that the invoice we sent you yesterday for $[Amount] did not correctly reflect the [X]% promotional discount we agreed upon during our call.

This was an oversight on my part during the account setup, and I apologize for the confusion. 

I have attached the revised, corrected invoice to this email reflecting the new total of $[Correct Amount]. If your accounting team has already processed the previous invoice, please let me know and I will issue a credit to your account immediately.

Thank you for your understanding!

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Subject: Notice: Scheduled maintenance on billing portal

Hi [Name],

We wanted to proactively let you know that our billing provider is experiencing intermittent downtime today. 

As a result, you may see a 'payment failed' notification if your subscription renewed today. Please ignore this notification. Your specific billing profile is secure, and your service will absolutely not be interrupted. 

The system will automatically retry the charge tomorrow once the maintenance is complete. I apologize for any confusion this automated email may have caused.

Best,
[Your Name]
tips: Be highly specific with numbers; state the exact amount they were charged and the amount being refunded.
If you made a mistake, fix it (e.g., process the refund) BEFORE you send the email.
Provide a clear timeline of when they can expect to see the funds return to their bank account.

faq.question: Should I offer compensation for a billing error?
faq.answer: If the error caused significant distress, such as triggering an overdraft fee for a consumer, offering a free month of service is a wise goodwill gesture.

faq.question: Who should send this email?
faq.answer: For enterprise B2B accounts, the email should come from their dedicated Account Manager or a high-level executive to show the matter is being taken seriously.

faq.question: Can I blame my payment processor?
faq.answer: No. The client does business with you, not your payment provider. Own the issue fully, even if it was a third-party glitch.

title: Informational Interview Request Email Templates
description: Ask for career advice the right way. Get more responses with these professional informational interview request emails.
intro:
An informational interview is an incredibly powerful networking tool. It allows you to speak directly with an established expert in your desired field, bypassing HR gatekeepers and building a genuine relationship before a job is even posted. However, executives receive countless emails asking to "pick their brain." To successfully secure a meeting, your request must be profoundly humble, complimentary, and clear that you are asking strictly for advice, not a job.

When writing an informational interview request, respect the recipient’s time above all else. Do not ask for an hour-long lunch. Ask for 10 or 15 minutes of their time via a phone call or Zoom. Begin your email by establishing context—how did you find them, and what specific achievement of theirs inspired you to reach out? If you have a mutual connection or share an alma mater, mention it immediately in the first sentence or subject line to warm up the cold email.

These customized templates will help you craft compelling, low-pressure requests. Adapting these proven scripts will dramatically improve your response rate, allowing you to access hidden job markets and invaluable mentorship from industry veterans.
templates:
Subject: Fellow [University] alum / Aspiring [Role]

Hi [Name],

My name is [Your Name] and I’m a recent graduate of [University]. Go [Mascot]!

I’ve been following your incredible career trajectory at [Company Name], specifically your work leading the [Specific Project]. I am currently looking to transition into a [Role] position, and your path from [A] to [B] is exactly what I aspire to achieve.

Would you be open to a brief 10-minute informational chat via Zoom next week? I would greatly appreciate a few minutes of your time to hear your advice on navigating this industry.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Subject: Loved your recent insights on [Platform/Topic]

Hi [Name],

I recently read your article regarding [Topic] on LinkedIn and found your point about [Specific Detail] incredibly insightful. 

I currently work as a [Your Role] at [Your Company] and am intensely focused on developing my skills in [Their Specialty]. Given your extensive expertise and leadership in this space, I was hoping to ask you a few brief questions about how you approach [Specific Challenge].

If you have 10 minutes for a quick phone call sometime next month, I would be incredibly honored to buy you a virtual coffee. 

Thank you for sharing your great content!

Cheers,
[Your Name]

Subject: Reaching out via [Mutual Connection's Name]

Hi [Name],

I was speaking with our mutual connection, [Mutual Connection Name], earlier today about my desire to pivot into [Industry/Field]. They highly recommended that I reach out to you, noting that you are one of the most respected minds in the space.

I would love the opportunity to briefly pick your brain regarding the current landscape of [Industry Field]. Specifically, I’d love to know what skills are most critical for someone transitioning into a [Job Title] role right now.

Do you have 10 minutes available next Tuesday or Thursday afternoon?

Warmly,
[Your Name]
tips: Never attach your resume to the initial request; it immediately makes it look like you are begging for a job.
Always suggest specific, short timeframes (e.g., '10 minutes next Tuesday').
Follow up every informational interview with a handwritten or personalized 'Thank You' email within 24 hours.

faq.question: Can I ask for a job during the interview?
faq.answer: No. The premise of the meeting was advice. If you ask for a job, you break their trust. Let them offer it naturally if they are impressed.

faq.question: What should I ask during the 10 minutes?
faq.answer: Prepare 3-4 specific questions about their career path, their industry predictions, and finally, ask if there is anyone else they recommend you speak with.

faq.question: What if they don't respond to the email?
faq.answer: Wait one full week and send a polite, one-sentence follow-up. If they ignore that, move on to your next prospect.

title: University Alumni Networking Outreach Emails
description: Leverage your college alumni network effortlessly to land interviews and build connections with these proven templates.
intro:
Your university alumni network is arguably one of the warmest "cold" audiences you will ever pitch. An alumnus possesses an immediate, inherent bias toward helping a fellow graduate from their alma mater. Because you share common ground—from the campus traditions to the same frustrating professors—reaching out to an alumnus requires significantly less friction than a standard cold networking email. You simply need to leverage that shared history respectfully.

A powerful alumni networking email relies heavily on the subject line. By immediately proclaiming your shared university status (e.g., "Fellow [University] grad reaching out"), your email is almost guaranteed to be opened if they possess even a modicum of school spirit. Once opened, the email must quickly pivot to your professional inquiry. Keep the request low-lift: you are not asking them to hand-deliver your resume to their CEO. You are simply asking for 10 minutes of situational advice or an informal chat about their company culture.

Below are various templates designed for reaching out to alumni through email or LinkedIn. Customize them with your specific degree, graduation year, and career aspirations, while keeping the tone warm and collegial.
templates:
Subject: Fellow [University] Grad — Seeking brief advice

Hi [Name],

I hope you’re having a great week! I found your profile while searching through the [University Name] alumni network. Go [Mascot]!

I graduated in [Year] with a degree in [Major] and am currently exploring career opportunities within the [Industry] sector. I saw that you have built an incredibly successful career at [Company Name], and I was hoping I might ask you a few quick questions.

If you have 10 minutes next week, I’d love to jump on a brief call to hear about your experience transitioning from [University] to the corporate world of [Industry].

No worries if your schedule is packed right now! 

Best,
[Your Name]

Subject: [University] Student reaching out regarding [Company Name]

Hi [Name],

My name is [Your Name] and I’m currently a senior at [University] studying [Major]. 

I’m currently navigating the post-graduation job search and [Company Name] is at the absolute top of my list of dream companies. Given your impressive tenure there as a [Title], I was hoping to learn a bit more about the day-to-day culture from a fellow alum.

Would you be open to a 10-minute informational chat in the coming weeks? I know you must be incredibly busy, so any brief advice you could share would be hugely appreciated.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Subject: [University Name] connection on LinkedIn

Hi [Name],

It’s always great to see a fellow [University Name] alum thriving in the [Industry] space!

I’m currently a [Role] at [Company Name] and am looking to pivot my career toward [Their Specialty]. Your background is incredibly inspiring, and I would love to connect to keep up with your journey.

If you ever need anything from the [Your City] area, do not hesitate to reach out! 

Cheers,
[Your Name]
tips: Always mention your shared university explicitly in the subject line.
Adopt a slightly more conversational, warm tone than a standard cold business pitch.
If you are currently a student, emphasize that. Senior alumni often love giving advice to current students.

faq.question: Is LinkedIn better than email for alumni?
faq.answer: Both work exceptionally well. LinkedIn is great for casual connections, while email is better if you are asking for a scheduled Zoom call.

faq.question: Can I use these templates if I didn't graduate?
faq.answer: If you attended the university, you are an alum, whether you finished the degree or not. You still share the campus experience.

faq.question: Will they get me a job?
faq.answer: Perhaps eventually. The goal of the email is to gain a champion inside the company who can refer you to the hiring manager once a role opens up.

title: How to Ask Someone to Be Your Mentor (Templates)
description: Find a great career mentor with these professional and polite mentor request email templates. Build lasting professional relationships.
intro:
Securing a dedicated career mentor can exponentially accelerate your professional growth, providing you with guidance, industry connections, and an objective sounding board for difficult decisions. However, you cannot simply cold-email a busy executive and demand, "Will you be my mentor?" Mentorship is a massive time commitment, and the relationship must be cultivated organically. If your initial request feels burdensome or one-sided, it will almost certainly be declined.

The secret to asking for mentorship is to never actually use the word "mentor" in the first email. Instead, you should ask for a highly specific, one-time conversation regarding a single piece of advice. By removing the pressure of a long-term commitment, the executive is much more likely to say yes to a 15-minute coffee chat. If that initial meeting goes brilliantly, you follow up and ask if they would be open to grabbing coffee once a quarter. This gradual approach organically builds the mentorship over time based on mutual respect.

The templates below are structured to initiate these high-value conversations gracefully. They focus on praising the potential mentor’s specific accomplishments, explaining exactly why you value their perspective, and asking for a manageable, time-bound interaction.
templates:
Subject: Inspired by your work with [Specific Project/Article]

Hi [Name],

I have been following your career for the past few years, and I was incredibly inspired by your recent work leading the [Specific Initiative/Project].

I am currently a [Your Role] at [Your Company] and am facing a career crossroads regarding [Brief description of challenge]. Because you successfully navigated a very similar transition early in your career, your perspective would be invaluable to me.

If you have 15 minutes to spare next month, I would be incredibly grateful to buy you a cup of coffee (or set up a brief Zoom call) to ask your advice on handling this transition.

Thank you for setting such a remarkable example in our industry.

Warmly,
[Your Name]

Subject: Quick question about your presentation on [Topic]

Hi [Name],

I attended the [Event Name] conference last week and deeply resonated with your opening keynote regarding [Specific Topic]. 

I am currently tasked with implementing a similar strategy at my company, [Company Name], and I am struggling slightly with gaining executive buy-in for the initiative.

Would you be open to a 15-minute call sometime over the next few weeks? I would love to hear a bit more about how you initially championed this change at your organization.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Subject: Seeking career advice from a fellow [Industry] professional

Hi [Name],

I know you must be incredibly busy juggling your responsibilities as [Their Title], so I will be brief.

I am reaching out because I greatly admire the way you have built your personal brand and technical expertise within the [Niche] space. As a junior developer looking to specialize in [Niche], I am trying to determine which core skills I should prioritize this year.

Would you be willing to answering 2 brief questions over email regarding your own career trajectory in this space? I would greatly appreciate any guidance.

Best,
[Your Name]
tips: Do not ask 'Will you be my mentor?' on the first interaction. Build the relationship slowly.
Show that you have done your research by mentioning a specific, obscure project or article they wrote.
Always offer an asynchronous option, such as answering questions via email, if they are too busy for a call.

faq.question: How often should I meet with a mentor?
faq.answer: Once the relationship is established, meeting once a month or once a quarter is standard. Never overwhelm them with constant check-ins.

faq.question: Should I pay a mentor?
faq.answer: No. Genuine mentorship is built on mutual respect and a desire to give back. If you are paying for advice, you are hiring a career coach, not a mentor.

faq.question: What if they decline the meeting?
faq.answer: Graciously accept their response, thank them for their time, and ask if it would be okay if you occasionally followed their work on LinkedIn.

title: Professional Email Introduction Templates
description: Connect two professionals effortlessly. Use these double opt-in email introduction templates to expand networks and build social capital.
intro:
Introducing two professionals via email is one of the highest-leverage networking activities you can perform. By acting as the "super-connector," you instantly generate goodwill with both parties, cementing your reputation as an invaluable resource within your industry. However, a poorly executed introduction can create awkwardness and unwanted obligations. A professional introduction must be executed with precision and a strict adherence to the "Double Opt-In" rule.

Before ever connecting two busy people, you must secure permission from both parties privately—this is the Double Opt-In. Ask Person A if they would like to meet Person B, and separately ask Person B the same question. Once both have enthusiastically agreed, you send the official introduction email. The final email should be incredibly brief: introduce both parties, briefly state the specific reason you are connecting them, clearly highlight how they can mutually benefit each other, and gracefully exit the thread so they can coordinate privately.

The templates provided below cover scenarios ranging from introducing two founders, connecting a candidate with a hiring manager, or facilitating a vendor relationship. Use them to ensure your networking efforts are always viewed as helpful rather than intrusive.
templates:
Subject: Double Opt-In: Requesting an intro to [Target Name]

Hi [Target Name],

I hope you are doing well!

I recently caught up with my close friend [Requester's Name], who is currently building an amazing startup in the [Industry] space. Since you are deeply embedded in that sector, I immediately thought the two of you should connect.

[Requester's Name] would love to ask your advice regarding [Specific Topic]. Would you be open to me making an email introduction this week? No pressure at all if your plate is currently full!

Best,
[Your Name]

Subject: Intro: [Person A Name] <> [Person B Name]

Hi [Person A] and [Person B],

As promised, I am thrilled to officially connect the two of you!

[Person A], meet [Person B]. [Person B] is the brilliant mind behind [Their Company/Project] and has extensive expertise in [Niche].

[Person B], meet [Person A]. As I mentioned, [Person A] is currently tackling some incredible challenges over at [Company Name] regarding [Topic], which I knew you’d find fascinating to discuss.

I’ll let the two of you take it from here to schedule a time to chat. I am stepping off this thread, but I hope you guys have a fantastic conversation!

Cheers,
[Your Name]

Subject: [Your Name] / [Hiring Manager Name] — Introduction

Hi [Hiring Manager],

I want to introduce you to [Candidate's Name], whom you are copied on this email.

[Candidate's Name] and I worked together at [Previous Company] for 3 years, where they single-handedly managed the [Specific Project] to massive success. When I saw you were hiring for the [Open Role] position, I immediately thought they would be an absolutely perfect fit for your team culture.

[Candidate's Name], I’ll let you take it from here to share a bit more about your background with [Hiring Manager].

Best of luck to you both!
[Your Name]
tips: Always use the Double Opt-In rule to respect everyone’s inbox.
Make the subject line obvious, ideally using the 'Intro: Name <> Name' format.
Explicitly state 'I will let you take it from here' so they know you don't expect to be copied on the replies.

faq.question: What is a 'forwardable email'?
faq.answer: If you want an intro, write an email to your mutual connection that is fully self-contained. Your connection can simply click 'Forward' to the target, saving them the effort of writing the pitch themselves.

faq.question: How do I reply to an introduction email?
faq.answer: Reply All. Immediately thank the connector, move them to the BCC line to spare their inbox, and directly engage the new contact to schedule a call.

faq.question: Should I follow up with the person who introduced us?
faq.answer: Yes! A few weeks later, shoot the connector a quick email saying 'Thanks again for introducing me to [Name], we had a great chat!'

title: LinkedIn Recommendation Request Emails
description: Boost your LinkedIn profile by requesting professional recommendations from colleagues and managers using these polite templates.
intro:
A robust LinkedIn profile is essential for modern career advancement, and high-quality written recommendations are the ultimate form of social proof. A strong recommendation from a former manager or respected colleague powerfully validates the skills listed on your resume. However, writing a glowing, articulate recommendation requires significant time and mental energy from the requested party. To ensure you receive a rapid and high-quality response, you must make the process as frictionless as possible for them.

The secret to asking for a LinkedIn recommendation is providing them with a "cheat sheet" or a drafted outline. Do not simply ask, "Can you write me a recommendation?" and leave them staring at a blank screen. Remind them of a specific project you executed brilliantly together. Detail exactly which two or three soft skills you would love for them to highlight (e.g., leadership under pressure or technical agility). By effectively writing the core of the recommendation for them, you turn a 30-minute grueling chore into a 3-minute copy-and-paste favor.

Use these templates to ask a former boss, a coworker, or a client for a stellar LinkedIn testimonial. Tailor the specific project details to ensure the recommendation aligns perfectly with the jobs you are currently targeting.
templates:
Subject: Quick favor regarding my LinkedIn profile

Hi [Manager's Name],

I hope you’re doing incredibly well at [Company Name]! I still reflect fondly on the time we spent working together on the [Project Name].

I’m currently in the process of aggressively updating my portfolio and LinkedIn profile as I look to step into a more senior [Role Title] position. Since you directly managed my work during the [Project Name] rollout, I was hoping you might be willing to write a brief recommendation for my LinkedIn profile.

To make it as easy as possible, I’ve drafted a rough outline below focusing on my project management skills and our successful Q3 delivery. Please feel completely free to edit this aggressively or write your own!

[Insert drafted 3-sentence recommendation here]

Absolutely no pressure if you are too busy right now. I hope we can catch up soon!

Best,
[Your Name]

Subject: Reaching out for a LinkedIn Recommendation

Hi [Coworker's Name],

Hope everything is going great over in the [Department] team!

I’m currently overhauling my LinkedIn presence, and I’d absolutely love to feature a recommendation from you. Collaborating with you on the [Specific Task/Project] was a massive highlight for me, specifically how we managed to overcome the [Specific Challenge].

If you have 5 minutes this week, an honest recommendation highlighting my collaboration skills and technical proficiency with [Software] would mean the world to me.

I would also be more than happy to write a glowing recommendation for your profile in return! Just let me know which skills you’d like me to highlight.

Cheers,
[Your Name]

Subject: Thank you for the great project + A quick favor

Hi [Client Name],

It was an absolute pleasure partnering with your team over the past few months. I’m incredibly proud of the fact that we successfully delivered the [Deliverable] under budget and ahead of schedule.

As a freelance consultant, my business runs heavily on word-of-mouth and professional testimonials. If you were happy with my work on this project, would you be willing to leave a very brief recommendation on my LinkedIn profile?

Even a simple sentence or two regarding my communication style or the quality of the final deliverable would be incredibly helpful.

Thank you once again for your partnership.

Warmly,
[Your Name]
tips: Always offer to draft the recommendation for them; 90% of managers will gladly use it.
Offer to write a reciprocal recommendation for their profile in return.
Remind them of a highly specific win or metric you achieved together.

faq.question: Is it okay to ask for a recommendation if I left the company?
faq.answer: Yes, ideally you should ask within 2 to 4 weeks of departing, while your excellent work is still fresh in their mind.

faq.question: How long should a LinkedIn recommendation be?
faq.answer: Short and impactful. Three to four sentences focusing on one specific skill or achievement is far more readable than a massive essay.

faq.question: What if my current boss doesn't know I'm looking for a job?
faq.answer: Do not ask your current boss if it will jeopardize your job. Ask trusted coworkers or colleagues from previous companies.

title: Professional Sick Day Email Templates
description: Call in sick professionally without oversharing. Use these quick sick day email templates to notify your boss and team.
intro:
Taking a sick day is a necessary part of professional life, but emailing your manager to notify them can occasionally feel awkward or anxiety-inducing. The key to a professional sick day email is brevity. You do not need to over-explain your symptoms or justify your illness; you simply need to communicate your absence clearly, state your availability for urgent matters, and ensure your immediate responsibilities are covered.

A great sick day email should be sent as early in the morning as possible. Begin by clearly stating that you will be taking a sick day and will not be online. Next, proactively mention how urgent matters will be handled in your absence—whether you are delegating a specific task to a coworker or stating that you will monitor your inbox strictly for emergencies. By managing your workload proactively in the email, you eliminate stress for your team and prove your reliability, even when under the weather.

Use the templates below to appropriately notify your manager, your team, or your clients. They range from taking a complete digital detox for a severe illness to working a modified, remote schedule for a mild cold.
templates:
Subject: Sick Day - [Your Name]

Hi [Manager's Name],

Please accept this email as notification that I am feeling under the weather today and will need to take a sick day to recover.

I will be completely offline today and resting. I have already messaged [Coworker's Name] to ensure they can cover the 2 PM client call in my absence.

I anticipate being back online tomorrow, but I will keep you updated. If an absolute emergency arises regarding the [Project Name], you can reach me via my cell.

Best,
[Your Name]

Subject: Working remotely today / Feeling unwell

Hi [Team],

I woke up feeling quite under the weather today, so I will not be coming into the office.

I am well enough to monitor my inbox and keep the [Project Name] moving forward remotely, but I will be skipping all non-essential video meetings today so I can rest.

If you need immediate assistance with anything urgent, please ping me on Slack. Otherwise, slower response times are likely today.

Thanks for understanding,
[Your Name]

Subject: Out of Office: Sick Day

Hi [Client Name],

I’m writing to let you know that I am unexpectedly out of the office today due to illness.

Because I want to ensure my team addresses your needs promptly, I have CC’d [Colleague's Name] on this email. They are fully briefed on our ongoing project and can assist you with any immediate questions today.

I will review your file as soon as I return tomorrow and respond to you directly.

Thank you for your patience,
[Your Name]
tips: Keep the email extremely brief; do not overshare details about your physical symptoms.
Send the email as early as possible before the start of the typical workday.
If you are taking a mental health day, use the exact same template and simply say 'I am taking a sick day today'.

faq.question: Should I text or email my boss?
faq.answer: Follow your company's established norms. In most corporate environments, an email or Slack message is standard, while retail/shift jobs often require a phone call.

faq.question: Do I need to check email while sick?
faq.answer: If you take a full sick day, you should absolutely disconnect to recover. If you are just a bit sniffly but working, communicate that you are 'working slower than usual'.

faq.question: Is it okay to take a sick day for mental exhaustion?
faq.answer: Yes. Mental exhaustion leads to burnout. You do not need a fever to utilize your legally provided sick time. Just say you are 'unwell'.

title: Professional Resignation Letter Email Templates
description: Leave your job gracefully. Protect your professional reputation with these formal resignation letter email templates.
intro:
Resigning from a job is a monumental career transition. While you may feel excitement, anxiety, or even relief, your resignation letter must remain entirely objective, professional, and graceful. The corporate world is astonishingly small; burning a bridge during your exit can severely damage your long-term reputation and network. Your resignation email serves as the formal, legal documentation of your departure and should reflect the highest standard of professionalism.

A flawless resignation email contains four necessary elements: a clear statement of resignation, the exact date of your final day (typically giving the standard two weeks' notice), an expression of gratitude for the opportunity, and a firm commitment to ensuring a smooth handover of your responsibilities. Avoid airing grievances, complaining about management, or bragging about your new position. The resignation letter goes into your permanent HR file, so keep it meticulously polite and legally concise.

The templates provided below cover various resignation scenarios, including the standard two weeks' notice, a resignation requiring immediate departure due to extreme circumstances, and a warm farewell to a trusted mentor. Use them to finalize your departure with dignity.
templates:
Subject: Resignation - [Your Name]

Dear [Manager's Name],

Please accept this email as formal notification that I am resigning from my position as [Your Title] at [Company Name]. My last day of employment will be [Date, typically 2 weeks out].

I am incredibly grateful for the opportunities I’ve had to grow and contribute to the team over the past [Time Period]. I have learned so much from your leadership and have truly enjoyed collaborating with the incredible team here.

Over the next two weeks, I am fully committed to ensuring a smooth transition. I will finalize my current tasks on the [Project Name] and create a detailed handover document for the team.

I wish the company continued success in the future.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

Subject: Formal Resignation: [Your Name] - [Role]

Dear [HR Name / Manager],

I am writing to formally notify you of my resignation from [Company Name], effective [Date].

Thank you for the support and professional development you have provided me during my tenure. I am proud of the work we accomplished together, particularly our success on [Brief Project Mention].

I am dedicated to making this transition as seamless as possible and will spend my remaining time wrapping up my duties and briefing my colleagues on outstanding client accounts.

Thank you again for the opportunity to work here.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Subject: Immediate Resignation - [Your Name]

Dear [Manager's Name],

Please accept this letter as formal notification that I am resigning from my position as [Title] at [Company], effective immediately today, [Date].

Due to unforeseen personal circumstances, I am unable to fulfill the standard notice period. I apologize for the abrupt nature of my departure and the inconvenience this will cause the team.

I will return all company equipment to the IT department by tomorrow morning. Please let me know how we can swiftly process my final payroll and exit documentation.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
tips: Always resign to your direct manager verbally (or via Zoom) first before sending the formal HR email.
Keep the letter unemotional and brief. This is a legal HR document.
Never vent your frustrations in the resignation letter; save constructive feedback for the exit interview if necessary.

faq.question: Do I have to give two weeks' notice?
faq.answer: Two weeks is the professional standard in the US, but it is a courtesy, unless stated otherwise in a binding employment contract.

faq.question: Should I say where I am going next?
faq.answer: No. You are under no obligation to disclose your next employer. Simply state you are 'moving on to a new opportunity'.

faq.question: What if they ask me to leave immediately?
faq.answer: Some companies will accept your resignation and walk you out immediately for security reasons. Always ensure your personal files are removed before resigning.

title: How to Ask for a Promotion via Email
description: Secure your next career step. Use these strategic templates to ask for a promotion or a raise based on undeniable merit and data.
intro:
Asking for a promotion or a salary raise is arguably the most intimidating email you will write in your career. Many professionals wait passively for management to recognize their hard work, resulting in years of stagnated career growth. To secure a promotion, you must advocate for yourself aggressively but professionally. You must build a rock-solid, data-driven business case proving that the value you currently deliver far exceeds your current title and compensation.

The most fatal mistake when requesting a promotion is basing your argument on personal needs (e.g., "My rent went up") or tenure (e.g., "I've been here a year"). Management promotes based on business value. Your email request should not aggressively demand an immediate title change; rather, it should formally request a dedicated meeting to discuss your career trajectory. The email must briefly highlight two or three massive wins you recently delivered—emphasizing revenue generated, costs saved, or major systems built—to justify the requested meeting.

Use these customized templates to initiate the promotion conversation. Outline your accomplishments clearly, demonstrate that you are already operating at the level of the desired role, and ask for a formal review to discuss your future alignment with the company.
templates:
Subject: Discussing my career trajectory at [Company Name]

Hi [Manager's Name],

I hope you’re having a great week.

As my one-year work anniversary approaches, I’ve been reflecting on the incredible growth our team has experienced. Over the past 12 months, I’m particularly proud of having led the [Project Name] which resulted in a [X]% increase in [Metric], and taking on additional managerial responsibilities regarding the new software rollout.

Given the expanded scope of my responsibilities and my consistent track record of exceeding my KPIs, I would love to schedule a dedicated 30-minute meeting to discuss my career trajectory here and the possibility of transitioning into a [Desired Title] role.

Could we put 30 minutes on the calendar next week for this conversation?

Best,
[Your Name]

Subject: Check-in: [Desired Role] position

Hi [Manager's Name],

I’m writing to you today to formally express my immense interest in the open [Open Position Title] role within our department.

For the past 18 months, I have been actively shadowing the responsibilities of this role. Specifically, I have completely managed the [Client Account/Process] and consistently delivered a [Metric] improvement in efficiency quarter over quarter.

I genuinely believe my deep understanding of our internal systems and my proven dedication to the team make me a perfect fit for this elevated role. 

I would love the opportunity to formally interview for this position. Do you have time on Friday to discuss my candidacy?

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Subject: Salary review request - [Your Name]

Hi [Manager's Name],

I’m incredibly proud of the significant milestones we hit during Q3, specifically the successful launch of [Project Name] under budget.

Since taking over the management of [Specific Duty] six months ago, my responsibilities have grown substantially beyond the original scope of my [Current Title] role.

I would love to schedule a meeting to review my recent performance and discuss adjusting my baseline compensation to better align with my current contributions and current market rates for this level of output.

Please let me know when you might be available to discuss this.

Best,
[Your Name]
tips: Never send an email demanding a promotion; use the email to pitch the meeting, and make your case in person/on Zoom.
Bring a one-page 'brag sheet' to the meeting containing data regarding your achievements.
Frame the conversation around how your promotion helps the company achieve its goals.

faq.question: When is the best time to ask for a promotion?
faq.answer: The absolute best time is immediately after you have delivered a massive win or successfully completed a high-visibility project.

faq.question: What if they say no?
faq.answer: Ask them exactly what specific, measurable goals you need to hit in the next 6 months to secure the promotion. Get it in writing.

faq.question: Should I mention I have another offer to force a raise?
faq.answer: This is high-risk. While it can force an immediate counter-offer, it severely damages trust and marks you as a flight risk to management.

title: Out of Office (OOO) Autoresponder Templates
description: Set professional boundaries while on vacation. Use these tailored OOO autoresponder templates for vacations, maternity leave, and holidays.
intro:
Setting an Out of Office (OOO) autoresponder is the ultimate act of professional boundary-setting. When you step away from work for a vacation, a medical leave, or a major holiday, you must give yourself permission to completely disconnect. An overly vague OOO message leads to confused clients and coworkers who may expect an immediate reply. A flawlessly executed OOO message manages expectations automatically, protecting your peace of mind while ensuring the business continues running.

A great autoresponder is explicitly clear about three things: the exact date you left, the exact date you will return and begin reading emails again, and crucially, who the sender should contact in case of an absolute catastrophe. Do not promise to "check email intermittently" unless you actually plan to work during your vacation. If you promise to check in, colleagues will treat your vacation as a minor inconvenience rather than a hard boundary.

The templates below cover multiple scenarios, from a standard one-week beach vacation to an extensive month-long sabbatical or maternity leave. Set them up, turn off your notifications, and enjoy your time away from the screen.
templates:
Subject: Out of Office: [Your Name]

Hi there,

Thanks for reaching out! I am currently out of the office on annual leave with no access to email. I will return to the office on [Date of Return].

If your request is non-urgent, I will review it upon my return and get back to you as soon as possible.

If you require immediate assistance before [Date of Return], please contact my colleague, [Colleague's Name], at [Colleague's Email], and they will be happy to assist you.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Subject: OOO: Attending [Conference Name]

Hello,

Thank you for your email. I am currently out of the office attending the [Conference Name] in [City] from [Start Date] through [End Date].

I will be checking email intermittently in the evenings, but please expect significantly delayed responses.

If your issue is urgent and requires immediate attention, please forward this email to [Manager/Team Email] with the word 'URGENT' in the subject line.

Thank you for understanding!

Best,
[Your Name]

Subject: Out of Office: Extended Leave

Hello,

Thank you for reaching out. I am currently on an extended medical/parental leave and will be entirely disconnected from email until my projected return on [Month/Year].

During my absence, my inbox is not being actively monitored. 

For any inquiries regarding [Project/Task A], please contact [Name] at [Email].
For all other matters, please reach out directly to my manager, [Manager's Name], at [Manager's Email].

I look forward to connecting with you upon my return.

Warmly,
[Your Name]
tips: Always set an explicit return date so the sender knows exactly when to expect a reply.
Clear the emergency contact person beforehand. Never put a colleague's email in your OOO message without asking them first.
Delete phrases like 'I will check email occasionally' if you want a true vacation.

faq.question: Should my internal and external OOO messages be different?
faq.answer: Yes, many email clients allow this. Your internal message can be casual ('I'm camping, ask Steve'), while the external should be highly professional.

faq.question: Do I need an OOO for just one sick day?
faq.answer: Yes, an autoresponder for a sick day instantly manages expectations and prevents clients from feeling ignored.

faq.question: Should I include details about where I am traveling?
faq.answer: Generally, no. Keep it strictly professional. Telling clients you are 'sipping margaritas in Cancun' is rarely necessary.

title: Farewell Email to Coworkers (Goodbye Templates)
description: Leave a lasting positive impression. Say goodbye to your colleagues with these warm, professional farewell email templates.
intro:
Your final day at a company is just as important as your first. Sending a thoughtful farewell email to your coworkers solidifies your professional network, leaves a lasting positive impression, and provides your colleagues with a way to stay in touch as your career progresses.
